findings.description: The proposal and design documents are truncated, missing full endpoint specifications for the new coordinator endpoints and the complete implementation plan (ends at 'blo' and 're').

The interaction model for 'drag-to-Ready' via PATCH /issues/{id}/labels is ambiguous. If this is intended to transition tasks to the 'In Flight' column, it should trigger a formal state transition (e.g., 'claim') rather than just a metadata update.

Destructive operations like DELETE /locks and POST /agents/{id}/kick lack a defined authorization model beyond 'short-lived JWT'. The source of truth for operator identity and permission levels must be specified.

The 'Done' column filter is hardcoded to 'last 24h'. While acceptable for MVP, it lacks a mechanism for viewing older history or pagination, which may lead to operator confusion about 'missing' completed tasks.

The design specifies POST /issues/list for resource retrieval. Using POST for read-only listing deviates from standard REST patterns and may prevent browser-level optimizations (caching, prefetching) or simple bookmarking.
